name: MorphoBlue
report: The exploit stemmed from an oracle misconfiguration that caused the price of PAXG to be inflated by a factor of 10^12 due to a decimal mismatch between USDC (6 decimals) and PAXG (18 decimals). The attacker deposited only $350 worth of PAXG and borrowed $250,000 in USDC by exploiting the highly overvalued asset. The root cause was the incorrect configuration of the oracle's SCALE_FACTOR, which led to a false price feed that the borrowing logic relied upon without sufficient validation.
